policy_statement: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments on a six-yearly cycle after each national risk assessment.
mechanism_description: It will update guidance for local climate change risk assessments on a regular cycle, improving the information base used to plan and prioritize adaptation actions.
primary_causal_pathway: Improved climate-risk information/governance → better adaptation planning & prioritization → ↓ exposure/vulnerability to climate hazards → ↓ climate impact damages
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Changed activity/system: Local governments’ risk assessment practice and planning inputs are upgraded via regularly updated guidance (methods, data, scenarios, standards).  
(2) Mediating process: More accurate/consistent hazard, exposure, and vulnerability assessment improves identification of high-risk assets/populations and the cost-effectiveness ranking of adaptation options.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Better-targeted and timelier adaptation measures are selected and implemented, reducing expected losses from climate hazards (e.g., flooding, heat, coastal inundation).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is improving adaptation decisions that reduce exposure and vulnerability to climate hazards (thereby reducing climate impact damages).
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Governance intervention improves climate-risk assessment quality, which improves adaptation planning decisions, leading to reduced exposure/vulnerability and reduced damages.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: The mechanism does not directly reduce GHG emissions (Mitigation) nor primarily target energy/material efficiency (Resource Efficiency) or ecosystems as the main lever (Nature-Based Solutions); its core function is enabling adaptation action selection and prioritization.  
3) Edge cases applied: If guidance explicitly mandates incorporating mitigation (e.g., emissions budgeting) it could support Mitigation as a co-benefit, but the dominant effect remains adaptation planning support because the content is climate risk assessment guidance.  
4) Applies to ALL instances of this mechanism: Any policy that updates/maintains guidance for local climate risk assessments functions as an adaptation-enabling measure improving planning inputs to reduce climate risk, regardless of jurisdiction.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Any finer categorization (e.g., “capacity building,” “risk governance,” “information services”) should be assigned at the individual policy level based on specific implementation details and links to downstream actions.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Could have mitigation co-benefits if the updated guidance influences land-use/infrastructure choices that also lower emissions, but the dominant pathway is adaptation enablement via improved risk information and planning.
key_indicators: "guide to local climate change risk assessments"; "Sets out a step-by-step process"; "will be updated on a six-yearly cycle"; "after the release of each national climate change risk assessment"
co_benefits: Improved general disaster risk management and local planning quality (beyond climate-specific risks)
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be interpreted as an information tool (information-disclosure), but it is guidance for conducting assessments (a planning/governance strategy) rather than disclosure/reporting to the public or investors.
